marketFocus: UK CPI in line at 3.0% y/y, BoE April cut pricing remains on table
RBNZ holds OCR at 2.25%, signals possible Q4 hike but not fully priced
US-Iran talks show progress, crude falls as geopolitical premium eases
Fed officials signal prolonged pause, 75bps to neutral per Daly
FOMC Minutes due 20:00 FRA - key focus on inflation path and R-star views

USD.name: US Dollar
USD.summary: Dollar edges higher as Fed signals prolonged pause. Fed's Barr said outlook suggests steady rates for some time, while Daly noted 75bps to neutral. Goolsbee wants more evidence on inflation ebbing to 2%. FOMC Minutes due Wednesday 20:00 FRA. President's Day holiday thinned liquidity Tuesday.
drivers: Fed signals prolonged pause, 75bps to neutral per Daly
Barr: Outlook suggests steady rates for some time
Goolsbee wants more inflation evidence before cuts
Wednesday: FOMC Meeting Minutes
Thursday: Unemployment Claims, Philly Fed Index

EUR.name: Euro
EUR.summary: EUR consolidates below 1.1850 as ECB pushes back on dovish repricing. Lagarde remains committed to role, no departure decision made. German ZEW disappointed at 58.3 vs exp 65. EU ZEW 39.4 vs exp 45.2. Range-bound until fresh catalysts emerge. Friday: German Flash PMIs.
drivers: ECB pushes back on dovish repricing
Lagarde remains committed, no departure decision
German ZEW disappointed: 58.3 vs exp 65
EU ZEW 39.4 vs exp 45.2
Friday: German Flash Manufacturing PMI, Flash Services PMI

GBP.name: British Pound
GBP.summary: GBP under pressure after dovish UK jobs report Tuesday. Unemployment rose to 5.2%, wage growth slowed across both measures. Wednesday: UK CPI in line at 3.0% y/y, core 3.1%. BoE April cut pricing remains on table. El-Erian: CPI reinforces likelihood of BoE resuming cuts next month. GBP/USD testing 1.35 support.
drivers: Dovish UK jobs: unemployment 5.2%, wages slowed
Wednesday: CPI 3.0% y/y in line, core 3.1%
BoE April cut pricing remains on table
El-Erian: CPI reinforces BoE cut likelihood
Friday: Retail Sales

JPY.name: Japanese Yen
JPY.summary: JPY holds gains as carry trade unwind continues. Former BoJ board member Adachi sees reasonable chance of 25bps hike in April. BoJ March hike odds at 60% after Takaichi landslide win. USD/JPY trades 152.70-153.75 range. Carry trade disadvantage limits upside for CHF, supports JPY.
drivers: Former BoJ member Adachi: reasonable chance of April hike
BoJ March hike odds at 60% after Takaichi win
Carry trade unwind continues
USD/JPY range 152.70-153.75
Haven flows ahead of US-Iran talks

AUD.name: Australian Dollar
AUD.summary: AUD reclaims on hawkish RBA Minutes. Minutes reiterated uncertainty too high to commit to policy path, inflation would have been stickier without February hike. China away on holiday (Lunar New Year Feb 17-24). Thursday: Wage Price Index q/q - key catalyst. China stimulus speculation provides tailwind.
drivers: RBA Minutes: inflation stickier without Feb hike
Uncertainty too high to commit to policy path
Thursday: Wage Price Index - key catalyst
China Lunar New Year holiday (Feb 17-24)
China stimulus speculation provides tailwind

CAD.name: Canadian Dollar
CAD.summary: CAD under pressure despite soft CPI. Tuesday: CPI 2.3% y/y vs exp 2.4%, Trimmed Mean 2.4% vs exp 2.6%. Oxford Economics expects BoC to hold at 2.25% until early 2027. Trump considering exiting USMCA. PM Carney: talks on joining 'Golden Dome' going well. Crude falls on Iran talks progress.
drivers: Soft CPI: 2.3% y/y vs exp 2.4%
Oxford Econ: BoC hold until early 2027
Trump considering exiting USMCA
PM Carney: Golden Dome talks going well
Crude falls on Iran talks progress

CHF.name: Swiss Franc
CHF.summary: CHF consolidates as carry trade disadvantage limits upside. SNB's ultra-low rates (0.00%) make CHF less attractive despite safe-haven status. EUR/CHF stable near 0.95, lacks directional conviction. Geopolitical risk premium eases as US-Iran talks show progress. Safe-haven demand muted.
drivers: Carry trade disadvantage limits upside
SNB ultra-low rates (0.00%) make CHF less attractive
EUR/CHF stable near 0.95
Geopolitical risk premium eases
Safe-haven demand muted

NZD.name: New Zealand Dollar
NZD.summary: NZD strengthens after RBNZ holds OCR at 2.25% as expected. Governor Breman: OCR trajectory aligned with economy, Q4 hike possible but not fully priced. Forward path reflects stronger economic outlook. Inflation expectations remain firm. Friday: RBNZ Gov Breman Speaks. NZD/USD ~0.6050.
drivers: RBNZ holds OCR at 2.25% as expected
Breman: Q4 hike possible but not fully priced
Forward path reflects stronger outlook
Inflation expectations remain firm
Friday: RBNZ Gov Breman Speaks

bonds.name: US 10Y Treasury
bonds.summary: T-notes settle marginally lower, curve flattens. Front-end rises (+2.9bps at 2yr), long-end falls (-1.6bps at 30yr). Fed signals prolonged pause dampens cut expectations. FOMC Minutes Wednesday key focus. 10yr at 4.044%, levels not seen since early December 2025.
drivers: Yields steady, curve flattens
Fed signals prolonged pause
FOMC Minutes Wednesday 20:00 FRA

bonds.name: German 10Y Bund
bonds.summary: Bunds firmer by a dozen ticks on weak German ZEW metrics (58.3 vs exp 65). ECB pushes back on aggressive dovish repricing. Lagarde remains committed to role. Geopolitical risk premium eases as Iran talks progress. German ZEW Current Conditions -65.9 vs exp -65.7.
drivers: Yields falling on weak German ZEW
ECB pushback on dovish repricing
Geopolitical risk premium eases

bonds.name: Japan 10Y JGB
bonds.summary: JGBs bid following dovish Japanese GDP. Former BoJ board member Adachi sees reasonable chance of April hike. BoJ March hike odds at 60% after Takaichi win. Carry trade unwind supports bonds. Haven flows ahead of geopolitical talks.
drivers: Yields falling on dovish GDP
Adachi: reasonable chance of April hike
Carry trade unwind supports bonds

bonds.name: UK 10Y Gilt
bonds.summary: Gilts outperform on dovish UK jobs/wages report. Unemployment 5.2%, just shy of BoE's 5.3% peak forecast. Wage growth moderated. CPI Wednesday in line at 3.0% y/y. BoE April cut pricing remains on table. March cut now priced at -21bps (from -20.3bps).
drivers: Yields falling on dovish UK data
Unemployment 5.2%, wages moderated
BoE April cut pricing on table
